name: Abstract Factory
summary: Create related families of objects through stable interfaces without binding clients to concrete product classes.

name: Adapter
summary: Convert one interface into another interface expected by a client.

name: Aggregator
summary: Combine related messages into one result once a completion condition is met.

name: Bridge
summary: Separate an abstraction from its implementation so both can vary independently.

name: Builder
summary: Separate the construction of a complex object from the representation that is produced.

name: Canonical Data Model
summary: Define a shared data representation that reduces pairwise transformations.

name: Chain of Responsibility
summary: Pass a request along a chain of handlers until one handles it or the chain completes.

name: Channel Adapter
summary: Connect a non-messaging application or resource to a messaging channel.

name: Channel Purger
summary: Remove unwanted messages from a channel to restore a clean operating or test state.

name: Claim Check
summary: Store bulky data elsewhere and send a reference through the message flow.

name: Command Message
summary: Represent an instruction to perform work as a message.

name: Command
summary: Encapsulate a request as an object so it can be queued, logged, retried, undone, or passed around.

name: Competing Consumers
summary: Run multiple consumers against one channel so work is distributed among them.

name: Composed Message Processor
summary: Split, route, process, and reassemble a compound message while preserving the overall flow.

name: Composite
summary: Represent part-whole hierarchies so clients can treat individual objects and groups uniformly.

name: Content-Based Router
summary: Choose a destination by inspecting message content.

name: Content Enricher
summary: Add missing data to a message before downstream processing.

name: Content Filter
summary: Remove unneeded data from a message before forwarding it.

name: Control Bus
summary: Use a messaging channel for operational commands and telemetry about the messaging system.

name: Correlation Identifier
summary: Attach an identifier that connects related messages in a conversation.

name: Datatype Channel
summary: Separate channels by message type so receivers know what kind of payload to expect.

name: Dead Letter Channel
summary: Preserve messages that cannot be delivered or processed after policy is exhausted.

name: Decorator
summary: Add responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it with another object that preserves the same interface.

name: Detour
summary: Temporarily route messages through extra steps for validation, testing, or troubleshooting.

name: Document Message
summary: Transfer a document-like data structure for another system to consume.

name: Durable Subscriber
summary: Preserve pub-sub messages for a subscriber while it is disconnected.

name: Dynamic Router
summary: Route messages using destinations that can change at runtime.

name: Envelope Wrapper
summary: Wrap existing payload data with headers or envelope structure required by the messaging system.

name: Event-Driven Consumer
summary: Invoke consumer logic automatically when messages arrive.

name: Event Message
summary: Announce that a meaningful fact or state transition occurred.

name: Facade
summary: Provide a simpler interface over a subsystem.

name: Factory Method
summary: Let subclasses or collaborators decide which concrete product to create through a stable creation method.

name: File Transfer
summary: Exchange data by writing and reading files through a shared transfer location.

name: Flyweight
summary: Share fine-grained immutable state to support large numbers of similar objects efficiently.

name: Format Indicator
summary: Include metadata that identifies the payload format or version.

name: Guaranteed Delivery
summary: Persist messages so they can survive process or broker interruptions until delivery policy completes.

name: Idempotent Receiver
summary: Handle duplicate messages without repeating unsafe side effects.

name: Interpreter
summary: Represent grammar rules as objects and evaluate sentences in that grammar.

name: Invalid Message Channel
summary: Route messages that fail validation to a dedicated channel for inspection or repair.

name: Iterator
summary: Provide sequential access to elements without exposing the underlying representation.

name: Mediator
summary: Centralize complex collaboration so peers do not reference each other directly.

name: Memento
summary: Capture an object's state so it can be restored later without exposing internals.

name: Message Broker
summary: Centralize routing, transformation, and mediation between message producers and consumers.

name: Message Bus
summary: Provide a shared messaging backbone with common contracts and integration conventions.

name: Message Channel
summary: Provide a logical path that carries messages from senders to receivers.

name: Message Dispatcher
summary: Coordinate message delivery from one channel to multiple local handlers.

name: Message Endpoint
summary: Connect application code to messaging infrastructure for sending or receiving.

name: Message Expiration
summary: Mark messages with a time after which processing is no longer useful.

name: Message Filter
summary: Remove messages that do not meet criteria before they reach downstream processing.

name: Message History
summary: Record the components or steps a message has passed through.

name: Message Router
summary: Route a message to one or more destinations based on routing logic.

name: Message Sequence
summary: Split related data across multiple ordered messages with enough metadata to reconstruct context.

name: Message Store
summary: Persist messages or selected metadata for audit, replay, analytics, or diagnostics.

name: Message Translator
summary: Convert a message from one schema or representation to another.

name: Message
summary: Package data and metadata as a unit exchanged through a channel.

name: Messaging Bridge
summary: Move messages between separate messaging systems while preserving useful semantics.

name: Messaging Gateway
summary: Expose messaging operations through an application-facing interface.

name: Messaging Mapper
summary: Map between domain objects and messaging representations.

name: Messaging
summary: Integrate applications by sending messages through channels with temporal decoupling.

name: Normalizer
summary: Transform different incoming formats into one common message shape.

name: Observer
summary: Notify dependents when an object changes without tightly coupling the subject to its observers.

name: Pipes and Filters
summary: Process data through independent steps connected by channels.

name: Point-to-Point Channel
summary: Deliver each message to one eligible receiver.

name: Polling Consumer
summary: Let an application request messages when it is ready to process them.

name: Process Manager
summary: Coordinate a multi-step message-driven process whose next actions depend on state and events.

name: Prototype
summary: Create new objects by copying an existing configured instance.

name: Proxy
summary: Control access to another object through a stand-in with the same interface.

name: Publish-Subscribe Channel
summary: Broadcast messages to multiple interested subscribers.

name: Recipient List
summary: Route one message to a computed list of recipients.

name: Remote Procedure Invocation
summary: Expose application behavior through synchronous remote calls.

name: Request-Reply
summary: Send a request message and receive a correlated response message.

name: Resequencer
summary: Reorder related messages into the expected sequence before forwarding.

name: Return Address
summary: Tell a receiver where to send a reply.

name: Routing Slip
summary: Attach the remaining processing steps to the message so it can move through a variable route.

name: Scatter-Gather
summary: Send a request to multiple recipients and aggregate their replies.

name: Selective Consumer
summary: Let a consumer receive only messages that match selection criteria.

name: Service Activator
summary: Invoke application service behavior in response to a message.

name: Shared Database
summary: Let multiple applications integrate through common database tables or views.

name: Singleton
summary: Ensure one logical instance exists and provide controlled access to it.

name: Smart Proxy
summary: Intercept request-reply conversations to preserve correlation or routing information across intermediaries.

name: Splitter
summary: Break one compound message into multiple messages for independent processing.

name: State
summary: Change an object's behavior when its internal state changes by delegating to state-specific behavior.

name: Strategy
summary: Define interchangeable algorithms behind a common contract.

name: Template Method
summary: Define an algorithm skeleton while letting subclasses or hooks customize selected steps.

name: Test Message
summary: Send known diagnostic messages through the system to verify behavior.

name: Transactional Client
summary: Coordinate message send or receive work with transactional boundaries.

name: Visitor
summary: Represent operations over an object structure separately from the classes in that structure.

name: Wire Tap
summary: Copy messages from a channel for monitoring, audit, or diagnostics without disturbing the main flow.

name: Event Fanout
summary: Notify multiple independent consumers that a business fact happened without coupling the producer to each recipient.

name: Legacy Boundary Adapter
summary: Isolate a legacy API, SDK, protocol, database, or message format behind a domain-friendly boundary.

name: Message Replay And Recovery
summary: Make failed or delayed message processing recoverable without corrupting downstream state.

name: Provider Abstraction
summary: Support multiple providers or engines while keeping provider selection, configuration, and domain behavior separated.

name: Request Reply Workflow
summary: Coordinate asynchronous work that still needs a response, without falling back to tight synchronous coupling.

name: Routing Pipeline
summary: Centralize routing and filtering decisions while keeping producers free from destination knowledge.

name: Stateful Workflow
summary: Make lifecycle transitions explicit when state-dependent behavior is scattered through conditionals.

name: Variation Point Refactor
summary: Extract a recurring behavior or construction variation without turning simple code into generic pattern machinery.

name: Anemic Facade
summary: A facade merely forwards calls while leaking subsystem concepts, errors, configuration, and lifecycle rules to callers.

name: Chatty Integration
summary: A workflow performs many small cross-boundary calls where one coarse contract, document message, or asynchronous exchange would be clearer.

name: Conditional Sprawl
summary: Repeated branches choose behavior, type construction, lifecycle state, or command handling across multiple modules.

name: Event Soup
summary: Many events circulate without clear ownership, naming discipline, contract governance, or traceable consumer responsibility.

name: Fragile Adapter
summary: An adapter hides a dependency name but leaks payload shape, protocol quirks, exceptions, or retry behavior.

name: God Service
summary: One service coordinates unrelated responsibilities, owns too much state, and becomes the default place for new behavior.

name: Hidden Router
summary: Routing decisions are scattered across producers, consumers, configuration, and conditionals with no single testable policy.

name: Lifecycle State Spread
summary: State checks and transition rules are scattered across services, controllers, handlers, or UI logic.

name: Naive Exactly Once
summary: The design assumes messages, commands, or jobs execute exactly once without durable idempotency, transaction boundaries, or recovery policy.

name: Overused Singleton
summary: Global instances are used for convenience, configuration, service lookup, or mutable process-wide state.

name: Pattern Ceremony
summary: Pattern-shaped classes, interfaces, or layers exist before the design has enough variation, ownership pressure, or runtime risk to justify them.

name: Provider Switch Sprawl
summary: Provider, engine, tenant, or region selection is repeated across services, handlers, or UI code.

name: Unbounded Retry
summary: Failed work retries indefinitely or with unclear terminal behavior, often without idempotency or operator visibility.

name: Adapter Boundary
summary: Stop external SDK, protocol, or legacy concepts from leaking into domain code.

name: Dead Letter Channel Recipe
summary: Give exhausted or permanently failed messages an owned, observable terminal path.

name: Idempotent Receiver Recipe
summary: Make a message handler safe under retry, redelivery, and replay.

name: Provider Bridge
summary: Separate provider selection and provider-specific SDK behavior from domain workflows.

name: Stateful Workflow Recipe
summary: Make lifecycle-dependent behavior and legal transitions explicit.

name: Strategy Refactor
summary: Move repeated algorithm-selection branches behind a domain-named behavior boundary.

name: Standard Architecture Decision Scorecard
summary: Compare pattern options with consistent decision criteria instead of relying on pattern familiarity.
